lens_summary: Real-world TUI usage during real working sessions. Behaviour across all four states (empty, single-source, multi-source, error-heavy) at narrow widths (~30 cols), under unfocus dimming, across a week of repeated use. Penalise sunny-mockup designs that hand-wave edges; reward calm peripheral-first designs that survive states. The 200ms-glance test governs.

calibration_against_seed_and_iteration_1.iteration_2_change_envelope: Strictly surgical. The bones, edges, and four state mocks are preserved verbatim from iteration-1. Three buildability stalls fixed at the spec edges: (a) Rule 0 system-tag precedence in §Source position; (b) splitOutcome bridge clause in §Verb-glyph column rule 1; (c) explicit column-arithmetic walkthrough in §States preamble. One deferred-honestly future-work item (same-agent multi-thread chip collision) added to Open questions. No new glyphs, no design-surface changes, no new mocks.
calibration_against_seed_and_iteration_1.expected_movement: hud-fidelity holds (no design-surface change, no chrome added, primitive ceiling preserved). four-questions holds (the stuck-vs-idle Pattern weakness from my iteration-1 phase-2 was scoped out of iteration-2 — the orchestrator explicitly framed iteration-2 as targeting the three buildability stalls only). terminal-feasibility lifts +1 because the three stalls land cleanly with one minor residual under-marking edge.

a_system_tag_precedence_under_multi_sour.evidence: Lines 214–218 introduce Rule 0 as highest-precedence: when entry.source startsWith '[', render a 1-cell tone-coloured glyph at column 1, never as eyebrow, never as 3-char chip. Explicit reuse of existing severity-glyph vocab entries (no new glyph budget). Crucially, the rule states that system tags 'do not participate in the agent-source run-length stream' — a [bell] between two pi db92 rows leaves the run unbroken. Line 218 names the failure modes the rule prevents (a fresh eyebrow on [bell], or the nonsense chip '[b│ ...').
a_system_tag_precedence_under_multi_sour.multi_source_interleave_behaviour: When [bell] arrives mid-multi-source, the chip stripe (cols 0–2) goes blank for that one row, the severity glyph occupies col 1, and pi/cc surrounding rows still chip-render because run-length-1 is preserved across the system-tag gap. The chip-column gap is itself diagnostic — it reads as 'a system event interrupted' the same way the verb-glyph blank-on-miss reads as 'non-tool event'. Self-consistent with §Verb-glyph column rule 4.
a_system_tag_precedence_under_multi_sour.lens_residual: The rule is unambiguous in prose but not visualised. A user who only reads the four state mocks won't see a [bell]-mid-multi-source row. For week-of-use memorability, a 5-row mock fragment in the rules section would lock the visual in. Not score-affecting under this lens (the rule itself is clean); flagged as a candidate ASI for iteration-3 alongside the under-marking edge.

b_splitOutcome_bridge_for_legacy_markers.evidence: Lines 188–197 cite the function (apps/tui/src/index.tsx:210), quote the regex /^(.*?)(\s*)(\((passed|failed)\))\s*$/ verbatim, and specify the bridge: when tone === 'error' AND splitOutcome.outcome?.tone === 'error', strip the (failed) suffix and rely on column-1 SEV_ERROR (no double-mark). For (passed), keep the existing tone-coloured suffix as the sole success carrier (no glyph; no displacement).
b_splitOutcome_bridge_for_legacy_markers.week_of_use_behaviour_passed_marker: Preserved cleanly. Legacy producers that emit 'Tests passed (passed)' continue to render with the green-toned suffix exactly as today. The user's learned rule 'green (passed) tail = success' survives the redesign untouched. This is the right call: success is the unmarked default; suffix is its sole carrier; no col-1 chrome is spent on the common case.
b_splitOutcome_bridge_for_legacy_markers.week_of_use_behaviour_failed_marker: Preserved for the AND-conjunction case. Legacy producers that emit '(failed)' WITH tone:'error' get the new behaviour: stripped suffix + SEV_ERROR col-1 stripe. The user's learned rule 'red col-1 stripe = exception' lands cleanly in error-cascade windows.
b_splitOutcome_bridge_for_legacy_markers.lens_residual_under_marking: Rule 1 keys ONLY on tone === 'error' (line 186). A producer emitting (failed) with tone:'neutral' (the runtime's splitOutcome at index.tsx:215 already detects tone from the suffix alone, independent of entry.tone — verified by the realist) would fall through to Rule 2/3, render verb-glyph + tone-coloured (failed) suffix, and silently miss the SEV_ERROR stripe that State 4 promises. Week-of-use impact: the rule 'red col-1 = exception' becomes producer-config-dependent — a real working-session hazard. One-clause fix: broaden trigger to (tone === 'error') OR (splitOutcome.outcome?.tone === 'error'). The bridge solved over-marking but not under-marking.

c_column_arithmetic_honesty_at_30_cols.evidence: Lines 296–304 explicitly state pad(1) | content(28) | pad(1), name that the leading space on most rows literally IS the left pad, and call out col-0 displacement when gutter (●) or chip head (pi│) appears. Resolves the iteration-1 ambiguity 'do mock rulers count chrome?'. The State-1 ruler reads 0–29 (30 cells) — verifiable.
c_column_arithmetic_honesty_at_30_cols.mock_walkthrough_30_cols: State 2 row '●r build.ts' = 11 cells, fits with 19 cells slack. State 3 chip row 'pi│ r scenarios.ts' = 18 cells, fits with 12 cells slack. State 4 freshest+failed '●✗ src/index.tsx' = 16 cells, fits with 14 cells slack. Sparkline rows ' ▂▃▄▅▆▆▅▃' = 9 cells, fits with 21 cells slack. All four states honest at 30 cols.
c_column_arithmetic_honesty_at_30_cols.lens_residual_off_by_one: The per-mode description budgets quoted in §Verb-glyph column ('≤24 cells at 30-col panel' for chip mode) and State 2 annotation ('≤27 cells' for eyebrow mode) imply description extends to col 29 (i.e. into the right pad). My computation against the explicit pad(1) | content(28) | pad(1) declaration says ≤23 (chip) and ≤26 (eyebrow). +1 off in both. The mocks themselves don't violate width — descriptions are pre-truncated and fit comfortably — but the truncation thresholds quoted in prose disagree with the panel-level rule by one cell. Cosmetic, not blocking. Realist's verified count was ≤22 / ≤25 (off-by-2 from iteration-1) — iteration-2 partially corrected toward the right number, partially away. A line that states the truncation budget exactly (≤23 / ≤26) would close it.

hud-fidelity.rationale: No design-surface change since iteration-1. Both terminal tests still pass cleanly: sparkline (8 cells × 8s/bucket, fixed ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█ alphabet, ▁-floor as visible baseline, no '7/min' label re-stating the shape) and spellcheck (gutter cell, verb glyph, SEV_ERROR all on the line of work). ≤3 primitives per row holds across both eyebrow and chip modes; the new Rule 0 keeps system-tag rows at 2 primitives (system-tag glyph + description). Data-ink redundancy still closed. The freshest+failed double-encoding (●✗ at cols 0–1) and the chip's │ separator on every multi-source row remain soft demerits keeping this off 10 — both already flagged in iteration-1 phase-2; iteration-2 didn't touch them. The candidate's surgical scope was correct: hud-fidelity wasn't where the iteration-1 lift gap lived.

four-questions.rationale: Liveness, Exception, Latest answered cleanly by glance. Pattern partially answered: sparkline shape carries burst/decay/flat coherently for the active case (visible across the three active state mocks), but the window-empty case (ii) — flat ▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁ — still renders identically whether the agent is wedged on a 60s+ Bash run or simply finished its burst. This is the lens-signature stuck-vs-idle gap that I flagged in iteration-1 phase-2 and proposed a Tier-3-dim-vs-Tier-2 fix for. Iteration-2 explicitly scoped to the three buildability stalls and did not address it; the open-questions list dropped it. Real working sessions hit this constantly (long compiles, slow tests, blocked subprocesses), and the user must context-switch to the agent's pane to disambiguate — the exact context-switch the activity zone exists to prevent. Holds at 8; not a regression, but the iteration-1-phase-2 weakness persists. This is my iteration-3 ASI.
four-questions.weakest_question_rationale: Stuck-vs-idle conflation under window-empty sparkline persists from iteration-1: a wedged agent and a finished agent both render flat ▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁. Cleavage requires either a sparkline-side Tier-step rule (Tier-3 dim for window-empty, Tier-2 for active-low) or an explicit cross-band reference to the rolodex severity spinner. Iteration-2 ships neither.

terminal-feasibility.rationale: Iteration-1's 8 was driven by the three buildability stalls explicitly named by the orchestrator. Iteration-2 closes (a) and (c) cleanly and (b) at 0.8 closure (over-marking solved, under-marking residual). The Rule 0 system-tag precedence is the cleanest of the three: branches first, reuses existing severity-glyph vocab (no glyph budget pressure), preserves source-run continuity across [bell], and self-consistent with the verb-glyph column-1 displacement mechanism — the rule fits the design's existing grain rather than adding a new layer. The column-arithmetic walkthrough makes the 30-col mocks auditable without re-counting; the +1 off-by-one in the per-mode description budgets is cosmetic. The splitOutcome bridge cites the function precisely, quotes the regex verbatim, handles the AND-conjunction case for double-mark prevention, and preserves the (passed) suffix as success's sole carrier (week-of-use behaviour for legacy success markers preserved cleanly). The under-marking residual (Rule 1 trigger keys only on tone:'error', not on splitOutcome.outcome.tone:'error') is a one-OR-clause fix and the right ASI candidate alongside stuck-vs-idle. Same-agent multi-thread chip collision is deferred-honestly with reasoning (forks across multiple design choices, benefits from user testing first) — acceptable for a surgical iteration. Holds short of 10 because of the under-marking residual and the deferred future-work item; both are realistic working-session edges that survive iteration-2.
state_intersections_undrawn: multi-source ∩ system-tag ([bell] mid-interleave — rule defined, mock omitted)
multi-source ∩ error-heavy (chip-mode error cascade — rule defined, mock omitted)

lens_observations.two_hundred_ms_glance: Holds for the four drawn states. Strengthened slightly under multi-source interleave because Rule 0 prevents [bell] from inducing a fresh eyebrow row mid-stream — the chip stripe stays stable, the eye keeps its anchor. Still fails for the window-empty stuck-vs-idle case: a flat sparkline reads the same whether the agent is wedged or done.
lens_observations.multi_source_thrash_resolved: Yes for distinct-agent run-length=1 with the iteration-1 hybrid rule. Now also resolved for system-tag interruptions: a [bell] in the middle of pi/cc interleaving doesn't break the run-length stream and doesn't waste a row on a one-time event. The chip stripe survives system-event peppering.
lens_observations.demands_attention_check: Sparkline calm. Gutter calm. Verb stripe calm. SEV_ERROR loud only on real exceptions. Chip column adds a 3-cell visual band on multi-source rows but it's structural, not salient. New system-tag glyph in col 1 borrows existing severity-bypass: it's loud only when the system event is loud (e.g. [bell] = waiting). No false-alarm primitive added.
lens_observations.consistency_focus_unfocus: Resolved in iteration-1; preserved in iteration-2. The per-primitive table is unambiguous; SEV_ERROR bypass + tier-slide for everything else produces the canonical 'dim landscape with bright red spots' HUD-at-rest signature. System-tag glyphs follow the same severity-bypass rule (tone-coloured, full intensity), which fits the unfocused-band pattern: the severity stripe is always bright whether or not you're looking.
lens_observations.memorability_week_of_use: Five verb glyphs are universal affordances (eye/list/magnify/pencil/play) — pre-learned. Closed dictionary + blank-on-miss means the column behaviour is stable across the week. The new system-tag rule reuses existing severity glyphs, so no new memorability load. Week-of-use rule for outcomes: 'green (passed) suffix = success, red col-1 stripe = exception' — clean for the AND-conjunction case but inconsistent for the under-marking edge (producer that emits (failed) with tone:'neutral' breaks the col-1 rule). The user learns the rule once and it works MOST of the time — the kind of intermittent inconsistency that erodes peripheral-trust over a week. This is the lens's most-cared-about residual.
lens_observations.intersection_edges_remaining: Three: (1) splitOutcome under-marking — Rule 1 trigger needs broadening to OR with splitOutcome.outcome?.tone === 'error'. One-line. Closes the week-of-use rule consistency. (2) Stuck-vs-idle Pattern conflation — sparkline window-empty Tier-3 dim vs active-low Tier-2 normal, plus an explicit cross-band reference noting that wedged-vs-finished is answered by the rolodex severity spinner above. Closes the four-questions Pattern weakness. (3) Same-agent multi-thread chip collision — deferred-honestly to future work; acceptable for surgical iteration but a real edge.
lens_observations.stuck_vs_idle_pattern_failure: Persists from iteration-1. The deliberation in my iteration-1 phase-2 lowered four-questions from 9 to 8 on this catch; iteration-2 chose to leave it un-fixed in favour of the three buildability stalls. The candidate's open-questions list also dropped it (only heading absorption / gutter gradient / same-agent multi-thread remain). For a single-focused iteration-3, this is the highest-leverage four-questions fix available.
lens_observations.system_tag_mid_stream_visualisation: Rule 0 is unambiguous in prose but not drawn. A real working session encounters [bell]-mid-multi-source weekly (every time an agent waits for input during parallel work). A 4–6 row mock fragment in the rules section would lock the visual in for week-of-use memorability. Not a score driver under this lens (the rule is clean); secondary candidate for iteration-3 if the ASI is split.
lens_observations.column_arithmetic_off_by_one: Cosmetic. The per-mode description budgets ('≤27' eyebrow, '≤24' chip) imply description extends into the right pad cell. My computation against pad(1)|content(28)|pad(1) yields ≤26 / ≤23. The mocks themselves are honest at 30 cols; the truncation thresholds in prose are slightly generous. A 1-line correction (≤23 / ≤26) would close it without disturbing anything else.
lens_observations.micro_typography: ●r and ●✗ adjacency note still missing — same gap as iteration-1. Likely fine via nerd-font intra-glyph padding but undocumented. Soft demerit; not blocking.
